title: stipa gallica
intro: Stipa gallica, also known as the meadow grass or little club grass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Stipa gallica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and fever to digestive issues and skin conditions.
